background: One component of the NIH-sponsored Women's Health Initiative study was a trial in which postmenopausal women were randomly assigned either estrogen or an identical-looking placebo. The estrogen-exposed group comprised n1=8506 subjects. The placebo group had n2=8102. Incidents of a combined index outcome (consisting of coronary disease, stroke, pulmonary embolism, breast cancer, endometrial cancer, colorectal cancer, hip fracture, and death due to other causes) were tallied in the groups. After a mean 5.2 years of follow-up, the estrogen-exposed group had 751 incidents and the control group had 623 incidents.

task.query: Calculate the cumulative incidence proportions for the estrogen-exposed group and the control group.
task.answer: Therefore, cumulative incidence proportions were p̂1=a1/n1=751/8506=0.08829 and p̂2=a2/n2=623/8102=0.07689.
